commit.message:
Corrige erro de pasta temporária no OCR para funcionar no Render

Problema:
- Multer estava configurado para salvar em 'uploads/temp/'
- Pasta não existe no Render, causando erro ENOENT
- Erro: "no such file or directory, open 'uploads/temp/ocr-*.jpg'"

Solução:
- Usa os.tmpdir() para pasta temporária do sistema
- Funciona em qualquer ambiente (local, Render, Vercel, etc.)
- Não requer criar pastas manualmente
- Sistema operacional gerencia limpeza automática

Imports adicionados:
- import os from 'os'
- import fs from 'fs'

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Amps gauge: switch to ideaspark ESP32+OLED board, add OLED diagnostics

- Amps now uses ideaspark board with integrated SSD1306 OLED display
- OLED diagnostic display behind #ifdef HAS_OLED: splash screen, two-zone
  layout (state + CAN count / gauge value + servo angle + CAN status + version)
- CAN pins changed to GPIO32/35 (proven on ideaspark, GPIO12 strapping issue)
- Pins: TX=32, RX=35, LED=14, Servo=27, SDA=21, SCL=22
- Fuel/Temp builds unaffected (all OLED code behind ifdef)
- CAN still blocked: suspected bad TJA1050 breakout board

commit.message:
refactor+test: Phase 4 — scene transition pure fn, animation/scripting integration tests

scene/mod.rs:
- Extract tick_transition(state, alpha, speed, dt) -> (TransitionState, f32) as pub fn
- Rewrite update_transition system to delegate alpha/state math to tick_transition
- Add 5 pure unit tests for the extracted function (fading_out, fading_in, idle noop,
  state boundary transitions)

engine/tests/integration_tests.rs:
- test_breathing_system_applies_scale_at_phase: spawn entity with phase=PI/2, verify
  scale.y > 1.0 on first frame
- test_blinking_system_triggers_on_expired_timer: spawn with timer=0.0, verify
  is_blinking==true after first update
- test_lua_context_basic_execution: LuaContext::new() + eval "1+1"
- test_lua_context_with_ffi_roundtrip: full FFI pipeline (register_core_api +
  register_generic_state_api, set/get float/string/bool via Lua)

ci.yml:
- Move CARGO_TARGET_DIR off tmpfs to runner home to avoid disk quota issues

Total workspace test count: 87

fix(cli): add fee inputs to withdrawal and exit commands

RequestWithdrawal and Exit used only Bond UTXOs as inputs with no
fee-paying input. When bonds are fully vested, fee = 0 (inputs =
outputs), rejected by mempool. Now selects a normal UTXO for fees
with change output, same dynamic formula as send/register/add-bond.

commit.message:
Fix Celery event loop bug: create async resources per task

The previous approach cached a CompositionRoot per worker process but
created a new event loop per task. Async resources (SQLAlchemy engine,
httpx client) are loop-bound and fail with "Future attached to a
different loop" on the second task invocation.

Fix: create a fresh CompositionRoot inside each task's asyncio.run()
call. All async resources are created and torn down within the same
loop. Uses asyncio.run() instead of manual loop management.

DoclingParser and ChonkieChunker model loading is handled by
HuggingFace's on-disk cache, so re-creation per task only pays the
model load cost on cold start.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Close hPSD_inner_int + hPSD_outer_int: 5→3 sorry's in CoulombConcreteTheorem42

- Extract psd_pointwise_bound_coulomb as reusable lemma in CoulombPSD.lean
- Add psd_outer_integrable_coulomb via Newtonian uniform bounds + inverse_poly_integrable
- Refactor psd_inner_integrable_coulomb to use extracted bound
- Remove dead code (hCg_nn), delete stale Aristotle input files
- Update critique.md, jobs.json, LOG.md

commit.message:
Add THREE International email campaign pipeline

- Create three-url-discovery.js for subdomain discovery (Common Crawl + Wayback + SerpAPI)
- Create three-scraper.js for HTTP-based contact scraping (no Puppeteer)
- Create email-campaign-three.js Cloud Function (12pm/3pm/6pm/9pm PT schedule)
- Add GitHub Actions workflows for automated discovery and scraping
- Add Firestore indexes for three_discovered_subdomains and three_contacts
- Add THREE to warming-config.json for dynamic batch sizing
- Update CLAUDE.md with THREE pipeline documentation

Pipeline extracts: email (mailto), phone (fa-phone), name (Facebook link or subdomain fallback)
Filters corporate emails (support@iii.earth)
252 subdomains discovered and ready for scraping

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: assorted correctness and safety improvements across subsystems

Combinators:
- hedge.rs: replace .unwrap() with .expect("timer is_some") for clarity
- race.rs: replace placeholder drop() macro body with compile_error!()
  to prevent silent misuse; mark race_macro_compiles_and_runs test as
  #[ignore] since macro now emits compile error
- select.rs: replace .unwrap() with descriptive .expect() message

Obligation/Lyapunov:
- Replace exact f64 == 0.0 comparisons with .abs() < f64::EPSILON
  for numerically robust convergence and component assertions

Record/Region:
- Fix try_close() to acquire inner write lock before quiescence check,
  preventing TOCTOU race between is_quiescent() and state transition
- Inline quiescence predicates under the held lock
- Call inner.heap.reclaim_all() directly under lock instead of
  self.clear_heap() which would re-acquire

Runtime/Kernel:
- Fix version compatibility check: use full SnapshotVersion comparison
  (SNAPSHOT_VERSION < reg.min_version) instead of major-only comparison,
  catching minor version incompatibilities
- Add test for minor version incompatibility

HTTP client:
- Minor formatting/style adjustment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Detect Alpha Vantage rate-limit response explicitly

AV returns HTTP 200 with an Information or Note field when the 25
req/day free-tier limit is hit, instead of the quote data. The old
code silently treated this as "no price data". Now we check for those
fields first, log a single clear warning, and throw a specific error
so prices.js falls back to the cached price for affected symbols.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): use PKCE flow for password reset emails

The reset password link was redirecting back to login because:
1. send-reset-email used createClient (implicit flow), which doesn't
   store a PKCE code_verifier cookie. The callback's exchangeCodeForSession
   couldn't complete the code exchange without it.
2. The callback's cookie transfer was dropping cookie options (path,
   httpOnly, etc.) when copying to the redirect response.

Fix: use createServerClient (SSR/PKCE) for resetPasswordForEmail so the
code_verifier is stored in a response cookie, and preserve full cookie
options when transferring to the redirect response.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(sync): correct notify_all wakeup logic and improve pool fairness

Notify::notify_all:
- Fix wakeup logic to only process active waiters with live wakers
  (waker.is_some()). Previously, the filter also matched already-notified
  entries (waker == None && notified == true), which could overwrite their
  generation and break the notify_one baton-pass-on-drop protocol.
- Simplify the filter_map to set notified=true and return waker.take()
  in a single branch

notify_bug_test.rs:
- Simplify test to focus on the core lost-wakeup scenario
- Remove redundant NoopWaker helper (use DummyWaker)
- Reduce test from 126 to 36 lines while preserving coverage

Pool (acquire fairness):
- Replace front-of-queue check with position-based fairness:
  compute waiter's position in queue and compare against available
  capacity (idle + remaining headroom) to determine eligibility
- Both acquire_idle_or_none and try_create_new use the same
  pos < available check, preventing queue-jumping
- Use position-based remove(idx) instead of retain() for O(1)
  targeted waiter removal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add structured verification system with pre/post conditions and actions

Replace LLRVerification with VerificationMethod, VerificationCondition,
and VerificationAction models. Conditions reference member variables by
qualified name with operators and expected values, mapping directly to
code assertions. Actions reference ontology nodes for the stimulus
between pre and post states.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add More tab with speaker profiles and indexing stats

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(transport): optimize load balancer single-select and avoid intermediate allocation

LoadBalancer::select_top_n: add early return for n==0/empty and
specialized O(n) single-element selection that avoids sorting.

LoadBalancer::select_single: rewrite per-strategy to iterate endpoints
directly without materializing a filtered SmallVec<[&Arc<Endpoint>; 8]>:
- LeastConnections: single-pass minimum scan
- WeightedLeastConnections: cross-multiply comparison avoiding division
- RoundRobin: count-then-nth with lazy filter
- WeightedRoundRobin: cumulative weight scan with inline filtering
- Random: unchanged (already optimized)

This eliminates a heap allocation per request for the common
single-endpoint selection path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix data routing: prioritize globalInputType over schemaType

The handleApply data fetch was checking schemaType === 'leaderboard'
first, which overrode the section's globalInputType. Templates with
globalInputType 'live-game' or 'player' were incorrectly routed to the
leaderboard fetch path. Reordered checks so effectiveType (from
globalInputType) takes priority. Also fixed wrong element bindings in
DB for Score Bug and Side Panel templates.

commit.message:
Fix prepayments: apply overpayments per-client instead of globally

Previous fix subtracted all PAID-deal overpayments from the global total,
causing prepayments to be -1.28B and net debt to go negative. Now each
client's overpayments from PAID deals are subtracted from that client's
own debt before splitting into gross/prepayments. Also includes clients
with only overpayments (no unpaid deals) in the prepayments total.

Continues the project-wide ambient time elimination into networking:

DNS Resolver:
- Add time_getter field and with_time_getter() constructor
- Cache TTL checks, retry cooldowns, and health tracking use injectable
  time source instead of Instant::now()
- Add deterministic test infrastructure and tests

Happy Eyeballs (RFC 8305):
- Add time_getter to HappyEyeballsConfig and HappyEyeballsState
- Connection attempt timing, interleave delays, and timeout calculations
  use injectable time source
- Add with_time_getter() builder method
- Add deterministic test helpers and timing verification tests

commit.message:
feat(database): replace ambient Instant::now() with injectable time source in DbPool

Both DbPool and AsyncDbPool used Instant::now() directly for connection
lifecycle timestamps (created_at, last_used, expiry checks, timeout
deadlines), making pool behavior impossible to test deterministically.

Changes:
- Add wall_clock_now() helper and time_getter field to both pool types
- Add with_time_getter() constructors and time_getter() accessors
- IdleConnection: rename is_expired/is_idle_too_long to *_at() variants
  that accept explicit Instant parameter
- All checkout, return, eviction, and warm-up paths use (self.time_getter)()
- checkout_with_retry deadline logic uses injectable clock
- Add deterministic test infrastructure: OnceLock-based test clock with
  AtomicU64 offset, lock_test_clock() for test isolation
- Add AsyncTestManager stub for async pool testing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
